issues.title: Bash shell integration emits unpaired OSC 133;A when PS1 is rewritten, breaking semantic-prompt-aware terminals (e.g. blank command output in Warp WSL sessions)
issues.next_action_reason: Brand-new, high-quality community bug with a concrete root cause and zero replies — acknowledge, confirm the emitter-contract bug, apply Area-AutoFix + Issue-Bug, and route to shell-integration owners. Do not request repro/version (root cause already supplied).
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks for the exceptionally detailed write-up, @acarl005 — the emitter-side contract problem is clear: `OSC 133;A` is emitted every prompt from `PROMPT_COMMAND` while `133;B` is appended once to `PS1`, so any framework or terminal that rebuilds/clears `PS1` (Warp managed-prompt, starship, oh-my-posh) drops the `B` and leaves an unclosed prompt zone. That's a real bug on our side, not just a Warp quirk, and the upstream cross-reference (warpdotdev/warp#13043) is helpful. We'll route this to the shell-integration owners for a fix that keeps the `A`/`B` pair emitted from the same mechanism. If you'd be open to sending a PR against `src/cascadia/inc/BashShellIntegration.h`, we'd be happy to review — otherwise we'll pick it up.
